description: Fix Discord error code 50014 (Invalid authentication token) being misclassified as a validation error instead of an auth error, which prevents token cache clearing and causes all subsequent API calls to fail until page refresh.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/discord && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Move Discord error code 50014 from VALIDATION_ERRORS to AUTH_ERRORS and clear token cache on body-level auth errors
userStories.description: As a user of the Discord plugin, I want error code 50014 (Invalid authentication token) to be correctly classified as an auth error so that the stale token is cleared from cache and I receive the correct error category for recovery.
acceptanceCriteria: Error code 50014 is in AUTH_ERRORS, not VALIDATION_ERRORS
When discordApi receives a non-ok response with body code 50014, it throws ToolError.auth() (not ToolError.validation())
When discordApi receives a non-ok response with body code 50014, clearAuthCache() is called before throwing, so the stale token is evicted from globalThis.__openTabs.tokenCache.discord
The AUTH_ERRORS body-level check in the non-ok response path calls clearAuthCache() before throwing, matching the behavior of the HTTP 401/403 fallback path
All other error codes remain in their current sets (50001, 50003, 50006, 50007, 50008, 50013, 50035, 50109 stay in VALIDATION_ERRORS)
cd plugins/discord && npm run build passes
cd plugins/discord && npm run type-check passes
cd plugins/discord && npm run lint passes
cd plugins/discord && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: plugins/discord/src/discord-api.ts

Two changes needed:

1. Move 50014 from VALIDATION_ERRORS (line 157) to AUTH_ERRORS (lines 133-137). Code 50014 means "Invalid authentication token" — the token itself is bad. This is an auth failure, not input validation. The comment on line 157 already says "Invalid authentication token" which makes the misclassification obvious.

2. Add clearAuthCache() call before throwing ToolError.auth() in the body-level auth error check within the non-ok response path (around lines 261-271). Currently, when discordCode is in AUTH_ERRORS at line 268, it throws ToolError.auth() but does NOT call clearAuthCache(). Compare with the HTTP 401/403 fallback at line 274 which DOES call clearAuthCache(). The fix is to add clearAuthCache() before the throw at line 269, so that body-level auth errors (including the newly-moved 50014) also clear the stale token.

Do NOT move 50001 (Missing Access) or 50013 (Missing Permissions) — these are permission errors where the token is valid but the user lacks specific permissions. Clearing the token cache for these would force unnecessary re-authentication.

The root cause of the practical impact: When Discord returns HTTP 403 with body {"code": 50014, "message": "Invalid authentication token used"}, the VALIDATION_ERRORS check at line 262 matches first and throws ToolError.validation(), skipping clearAuthCache() at line 274. The stale token stays cached in globalThis.__openTabs.tokenCache.discord, causing all subsequent API calls to fail with the same error until the page is refreshed.
